bloodAlcoholEstimator.description: Quick BAC estimate from standard drinks, weight and time.

meta.title: Blood Alcohol Estimator — Quick BAC Calculator with Standard Drinks
meta.description: Estimate your blood alcohol concentration (BAC) fast using standard drinks, body weight and time. Widmark formula, sobriety prognosis and legal limits. Free, instant, no sign-up.

bloodAlcoholEstimator.title: Blood Alcohol Estimator
bloodAlcoholEstimator.description: Quick BAC estimate using US standard drinks (14 g pure alcohol per drink), body weight and time — for a fast read on your level.
bloodAlcoholEstimator.instructions: Pick sex and unit, enter your body weight, the number of standard drinks you've had and the hours since your first drink. One US standard drink = 14 g pure alcohol (≈ 12 oz beer at 5%, 5 oz wine at 12%, or 1.5 oz spirits at 40%).
bloodAlcoholEstimator.aboveUsLimit: Above US legal limit (0.08%). Do not drive!
bloodAlcoholEstimator.aboveDeLimit: Above German legal limit (0.05%). Do not drive!
bloodAlcoholEstimator.effect_legal: Notably slowed reaction, coordination loss — US driving limit
bloodAlcoholEstimator.effect_significant: Slurred speech, blurred vision, severe balance issues
bloodAlcoholEstimator.howItWorksText: This estimator uses US standard drinks (14 g pure alcohol) and the Widmark formula. BAC (%) = (drinks × 14 g) / (weight × r) × 0.1 − 0.015 × hours, where r = 0.68 (male) or 0.55 (female). Elimination is approximately 0.015% per hour. Individual values can shift ±20–30% — stomach contents, liver health, medications and genetics all matter.
bloodAlcoholEstimator.disclaimer: This estimate is no substitute for a breath or blood test. If you've been drinking: do not drive.

faq.a: A US standard drink contains 14 g of pure alcohol. That's roughly 12 oz of beer (5%), 5 oz of wine (12%) or 1.5 oz of spirits (40%). UK units are smaller (8 g) — this calculator uses the US convention.

faq.q: What's the difference between BAC and promille?
faq.a: BAC in % (US) and promille (Europe) measure the same thing in different units. 0.08% BAC = 0.8‰. The result panel shows both side by side.

faq.q: How fast does the body process alcohol?
faq.a: On average about 0.015% BAC per hour (≈ 0.15‰). The rate is roughly constant and cannot be sped up by coffee, water or exercise — only time.
US: 0.08% BAC in most states (0.04% for commercial drivers, 0.00% under 21). Germany: 0.05% / 0.5‰ (0.03% with signs of impairment, 0.00% for new drivers). Most EU countries: 0.02–0.05%.
Stomach contents, liver function, medications, hormonal cycle and genetics all shift real BAC. Women on average have less body water. When in doubt, assume the more conservative reading.

faq.q: How is this different from the Blood Alcohol Calculator?
faq.a: The full Blood Alcohol Calculator takes individual drinks (volume × ABV) — precise but slower. This estimator uses standard drinks — faster but less fine-grained. Both use the Widmark formula.
